title: Calculation and evaluation of ratios
summary: 9706 P2 — profitability, liquidity, efficiency, and gearing ratios with interpretation.
learningObjectives: Calculate profitability ratios: GP%, NP%, ROCE.
Calculate liquidity: current and acid test.

simpleExplanation.title: Numbers that tell a story
simpleExplanation.summary: Ratios turn raw figures into comparable measures — profitability shows earning power, liquidity shows short-term survival, gearing shows debt risk.
steps: Profitability — GP margin, NP margin, ROCE.

flashcards.back: (PBIT ÷ Capital employed) × 100; CE = equity + long-term liabilities.

sections.content: **Ratios** (1.6.2) completes Section 1. Always **calculate → interpret → evaluate**. Use the interactive ratio diagram where available.

GP margin = $\frac{\text{Gross profit}}{\text{Revenue}} \times 100$

ROCE = $\frac{\text{PBIT}}{\text{Capital employed}} \times 100$

Current ratio = $\frac{\text{Current assets}}{\text{Current liabilities}}$

Acid test = $\frac{\text{Current assets − Inventory}}{\text{Current liabilities}}$

sections.question: Revenue $500,000; gross profit $150,000; net profit $40,000; PBIT $55,000; capital employed $400,000; current assets $90,000 (inventory $30,000); current liabilities $60,000. Calculate GP margin, ROCE, current ratio, and acid test.
sections.solution:
**GP margin** = (150,000 ÷ 500,000) × 100 = **30%**

**ROCE** = (55,000 ÷ 400,000) × 100 = **13.75%**

**Current ratio** = 90,000 ÷ 60,000 = **1.5 : 1**

**Acid test** = (90,000 − 30,000) ÷ 60,000 = **1.0 : 1**

**Interpretation:** Healthy GP margin; ROCE acceptable if cost of capital lower; liquidity adequate but inventory-heavy (acid test at minimum benchmark).
